visible_roles.purpose: prepare proposals, company introductions, and pitch material
build competitor and alternative comparison material

project_value: Useful future reference for packaging and selling an AI-Content-OS service or content product.
Launch, pricing, and competitor comparison should remain evidence-backed planning functions rather than generic agent labels.

items.design: warm off-white field, tiny orange symbol, one centered black/orange promise, one support line, one highlighted comment keyword
project_value: Reference for ending a detailed system carousel with one memorable summary and one action.
The visual quiet makes the CTA legible after many role-list slides.
items.guardrail: The employment metaphor is marketing language; the screenshot does not prove 34 installed, useful, or autonomous workers.

items.first_screen_impression: broad issue-scanning feed with restrained visual branding
cover_system: white centered headline near the lower portion
small SCAN IT label immediately above the headline
minimal graphic decoration despite varied topic categories
project_value: Alternative to the more urgent black-and-magenta news grammar: broader, calmer, and image-led.
Shows that a general issue account can preserve first-screen consistency using only label, headline position, and gradient.
Research pool for revenue sources beyond Coupang Partners.
Networks should later be compared by supported country, product quality, commission model, attribution window, payout, data access, API availability, and fit with existing account categories.
Naver BrandConnect remains especially relevant because local product data is already part of the project context.
items.guardrail: The list is not current enrollment, eligibility, approval, commission, or integration evidence; no affiliate enrollment or link issuance is authorized.

items.prompt_role: Naver Blog monetization-oriented SEO content writer
objectives: credible human-review tone without exaggerated promotion
voice_rules: include concrete lived-in details and modest imperfections
structure_start: required product or place name and target keywords
why the writer selected the subject
items.project_value: High-value prompt reference for a future Naver blog and commerce evidence pipeline.

body_requirements: first impression, package, appearance, usability, texture, scent, convenience, before-and-after impression
reason to choose versus similar products
gift, repurchase, value, or premium positioning when supported
media_plan: minimum eight to twelve assets when the source evidence supports that many
representative, detail, use, comparison, and before-and-after roles
do not force the count when media is unavailable or adds no value
claim_rules: do not state efficacy as certainty
avoid medical, treatment, and exaggerated expressions
avoid phrases such as unconditional recommendation, perfect product, or guaranteed effect
prefer bounded wording such as helped, felt manageable, usability was acceptable, or worth continued use when source evidence supports it

conversion_and_packaging_rules: mention repurchase intention or suitable recommendation target naturally
invite an appropriate comment, save, or purchase action without overstating urgency
produce five search-aware title candidates without clickbait
place the key search phrase toward the beginning when natural
format_rules: body target around 1,500 or more Korean characters when the topic warrants depth
include essential keywords naturally three to five times
keep paragraphs short enough for mobile reading
prepare Naver-blog-oriented hashtags as a separate output
items.project_value: Reusable prompt fragments for caption, blog, image plan, CTA, and compliance rather than one monolithic prompt.

observations: A long prompt is split into numbered thread entries rather than forced into one unreadable block.
Repeated context protects each continuation from losing the task requirements, but creates duplication.
A saved project prompt should instead use reusable sections and compose only the needed fragments at runtime.
project_value: Store voice, structure, media plan, claim limits, SEO, and CTA as independent prompt modules.
Generate a trace showing which prompt modules were used for each output.

visible_claims: Rust rewrite with faster file discovery, JSON parsing, aggregation, and large-session handling
TUI views for Overview, Usage, Models, and Daily
filtering across opencode, Claude, Codex, Cursor, and Gemini
GitHub login, leaderboard submission, and README usage cards
project_value: Potentially useful for understanding weekly token constraints and comparing model or session consumption.
Local read-only usage views are more relevant than public leaderboard or automatic submission features.
items.guardrail: The tool has not been checked for installation, compatibility, data access, privacy behavior, or correctness.

visible_claims: real-time quota bars across several AI subscriptions
warning that Claude Code may delete sessions after a default period
one-line settings change suggested to prevent session deletion
project_value: A bounded local audit could determine whether this helps prevent another token-limit handoff surprise.
If adopted, public submission and autosubmit should remain disabled unless explicitly approved.
Any session-retention setting must be checked against actual installed versions before modification.

cross_batch_learning: Sales, launch, pricing, and competitor comparison complete the earlier marketing role framework.
A calm image-led issue account and an urgent quote-led news account are different valid first-screen systems.
Affiliate diversification is a research and eligibility problem before it is an integration problem.
Naver commerce prompts should be modular, evidence-aware, human-readable, media-aware, and claim-bounded.
A long reusable prompt should be stored as composable sections instead of copied as one repeated block.
Token-usage tooling is most useful when local and private; public leaderboards and automatic uploads are not required for this project's need.
